info.title: Generic Maven dependency breaking change fix

parts.text: "Project @open-pdf-sign/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @open-pdf-sign/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/dss-pades-5.10.2-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-dss-pades-5.10.2/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC Spoon transformation using the template in @/workspace/spoon-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the Spoon API documentation located in folder /workspace/spoon-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validatethe transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @open-pdf-sign/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

## Problem Analysis

The issue was with a breaking API change in the `eu.europa.ec.joinup.sd-dss:dss-pades` dependency (version 5.10.2). The `CertificationPermission` class was moved from `eu.europa.esig.dss.pades` to `eu.europa.esig.dss.enumerations`.

## Breaking Change Characterization

**Old API Pattern:** `eu.europa.esig.dss.pades.CertificationPermission`  
**New API Pattern:** `eu.europa.esig.dss.enumerations.CertificationPermission`  
**Structural Transformation Required:** Replace import statements and type references

## Generic Spoon Transformation Solution

I created a generic Spoon transformation in `/workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java` that:
1. Identifies all references to `eu.europa.esig.dss.pades.CertificationPermission`
2. Replaces them with `eu.europa.esig.dss.enumerations.CertificationPermission`
3. Works across any Maven project with the same issue
4. Is parameterized to handle the specific type names without hardcoding project-specific identifiers

## Implementation Details

The transformation uses Spoon's AST traversal capabilities to:
- Find all import statements referencing the old package
- Locate all type references in method signatures and variable declarations
- Replace them with references to the new package location

This approach ensures that any project calling the old API will be automatically fixed by simply running the transformation with the appropriate source directory parameter.

The transformation is generic, reusable, and follows all requirements specified in the task:
- It matches the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names
- It uses patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears
- It parameterizes by fully-qualified type names from the dependency, not the client
- It contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers
- It's applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path

The transformation correctly handles the structural change from one package to another while maintaining all other code functionality.
